summarization.a: Figure 6 shows an image panel of three wafers mounted on a rotating plate, with each wafer divided into 10 equiangular sections. This segmentation is used to compute surface area–averaged pressures, surface coverage, and source generation and consumption rates, enabling parallel mesoscopic simulations within the multiscale modeling framework.

data_extraction.a:
|Description|
|-----------|
|Image panel showing three wafers mounted on a rotating plate. Each wafer is divided into 10 equiangular sections to evaluate surface area–averaged pressures, surface coverage, and source generation and consumption rates. The segmentation enables parallel mesoscopic simulations and area-weighted averaging for multiscale modeling.|

d.question: List the parameters evaluated for each wafer section in the simulation.
d.answer: Surface area–averaged pressure, Surface coverage, Source generation rates, Source consumption rates

b.question: Does dividing the wafers into multiple sections improve the accuracy of surface coverage estimation compared to treating the wafer as a single unit?
b.answer: Yes. Segmenting the wafer enables localized evaluation of pressures and reaction rates, leading to a more accurate area-weighted estimation of surface coverage than considering the wafer as a single homogeneous unit.

a.question: How are the wafers segmented on the rotating plate to facilitate multiscale simulations?
a.answer: Each of the three wafers is divided into 10 equiangular sections. This segmentation allows mesoscopic simulations to be executed in parallel for each section, and the results from all sections are combined to compute area-weighted averages of surface pressures, coverage, and source generation or consumption rates. This approach efficiently integrates the wafer-scale spatial information into the multiscale modeling framework.

c.question: How many sections is each wafer divided into, and how many wafers are present on the rotating plate?
c.answer: Each wafer is divided into 10 equiangular sections, and there are 3 wafers mounted on the rotating plate.
